title: Precise pin-operator fix with type coercion awareness
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.find_user_safe/1` that takes a `String.t()` user id. The schema's `id` field is `:integer`. Must safely parse the string to integer before pinning (so Ecto's automatic coercion doesn't mask parse errors). Use `case Integer.parse(id) do {n, ""} -> ..., _ -> {:error, :invalid_id} end`. On success, run `where: u.id == ^n` with the integer. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Ecto's pin coerces types silently; knowing to parse explicitly so type errors surface as application errors is advanced.
research_citation: Lasse Skindstad Ebert: 'It was simply ignoring that I had to do with a string and coerced it into a integer... I thought the pin-operator was only for inserting values of variables, but now I know it also acts as a coercer'
